steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The researcher claims some lakes show an increase in ice duration, contradicting the general declining trend from 1980 to 2006. The evidence must show at least one lake with more ice days in 2005–06 than in 1980–81.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'a single winter value for one lake': one data point cannot prove an increase over time. ✗
  • Choice B — 'a single winter value for one lake': same problem as A — no before-and-after comparison. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Lake Baikal: ~100 days in 1980–81, ~110–120 days in 2005–06': directly shows an increase across both periods, providing exactly the evidence the researcher needs. ✓
  • Choice D — 'two lakes increased': factually wrong — Kegonsa held flat and Oulujarvi declined; only Baikal increased. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
